title: ACP debug mode logged credential-profile request values
observed: The transport debug sink previously received complete ACP messages containing credential secretFields values.
failure_mechanism: Generic transport logging had no structured secret redaction.
break_it_angle: Enable ACP_DEBUG and create a profile with a sentinel secret.
impact: Credentials could enter developer logs, screenshots, or copied diagnostics.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Redact secret-bearing properties and values before console output.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Credential-profile secretFields is always fully redacted.
mitigation.behavior_test: The real console call omits a sentinel value.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One shared SDK sink covers all ACP debug calls.

non_goals: log raw credentials in any debug mode

title: Future-compatible workspace fields could preserve secret-shaped data
observed: Flattened unknown store fields were preserved without the import/export secret-field guard.
expected_boundary: Forward compatibility preserves benign metadata but never normalizes raw secret-shaped fields into workspace persistence.
failure_mechanism: The compatibility map bypassed canonical secret-shaped-field validation.
break_it_angle: Insert api_key with a sentinel and perform a store mutation.
impact: A future or malformed writer could cause secret material to persist in the workspace store.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Apply secret-shaped-field rejection to flattened unknown fields.
mitigation.behavior_test: Secret sentinel is rejected and absent from the store.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One store invariant closes every mutation path.

title: Concurrent profile writes could lose or misalign secure state
observed: Process-local locks and cached aggregate reads allowed two Config/service instances to overwrite or reorder profile state.
expected_boundary: Concurrent disjoint writes survive and profile metadata remains ordered with its secure-store operation.
failure_mechanism: Stale read-modify-write plus a missing cross-process transaction boundary.
break_it_angle: Release two pre-cached Config writers simultaneously and assert both final keys.
impact: Credentials can disappear or disagree with configured metadata, breaking authentication and recovery truth.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Serialize secure aggregate mutation and the complete profile metadata/secret workflow.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Retain pending deletion recovery for process interruption.
mitigation.behavior_test: Two concurrent writers leave both effects.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repair spans Config and workspace/profile transaction seams.

non_goals: distributed locking across different data directories

title: Workspace metadata entered the system context as instruction-like prose

evidence.quote: The JSON below is user-configured workspace metadata

observed: Imported/template-controlled strings were previously concatenated into an undifferentiated system-prompt prose block.
expected_boundary: Workspace metadata is represented as untrusted structured data and grants no authority.
failure_mechanism: Data and operational guidance shared the same instruction-like formatting.
break_it_angle: Import a workspace label resembling a tool instruction and inspect the session context/tool decision.
impact: Supplied metadata can steer model behavior, though it cannot grant a tool or replace backend session/profile state.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Serialize typed JSON and explicitly label every contained string as data.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Keep credential metadata out of context and retain code-enforced tool permissions.
mitigation.behavior_test: Rendered context is structured and contains no credential identifiers.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The workspace context has one backend renderer.

non_goals: treat prompt labeling as an authorization boundary

title: Workspace metadata could exhaust the session context

evidence.quote: workspace metadata must be at most {MAX_SERIALIZED_WORKSPACE_BYTES} bytes

observed: Unbounded folder/output arrays and strings were copied into durable workspace state and every session context.
expected_boundary: Model-visible workspace metadata has deterministic count, field, and aggregate bounds.
failure_mechanism: No resource limit existed at the canonical workspace boundary.
break_it_angle: Import thousands of folders or a smaller set of multi-kilobyte paths and start a session.
impact: Context exhaustion, elevated token cost, startup failure, or degraded behavior.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Cap collections, fields, paths, and total serialized workspace bytes.
mitigation.local_guardrail: Apply the same boundary to create/update/import/template/store reload.
mitigation.behavior_test: Over-count and over-size workspaces are rejected.

implementation_assessment.rationale: One canonical backend validator covers all persistence and context paths.
